catalysts.thesis: High-impact publication in NEJM validates Zorevunersen for Dravet Syndrome, significantly de-risking the asset. The market reaction (+1.8%) appears muted relative to the scientific validation, suggesting the catalyst is not yet fully priced.
catalysts.catalyst_details: Publication of Phase 1/2a and OLE data in The New England Journal of Medicine on March 4, 2026, showing substantial and durable seizure reductions.
catalysts.mispricing_evidence: Stock only up ~1.8% despite NEJM validation, which is typically a major credibility booster for biotech platforms.

catalysts.thesis: Reported a massive jump in Net Income ($457M vs $37M YoY) driven by divestments and core growth. The stock is digesting the news with mild volatility, offering a potential entry before the market fully values the strengthened balance sheet.
catalysts.catalyst_details: FY2025 Full Year Results released March 5, 2026, showing $457M Net Income and strong pipeline progress.
catalysts.mispricing_evidence: Trading at low P/E relative to new profitability levels; market cautious due to complex financial headline.
